checks.expectation: At least one of CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .cursorrules, or .cursor/rules/*.mdc exists at the repository root.
checks.gap: None. Both AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md exist at the root, with a deliberate primary/secondary split.

checks.expectation: The primary file is at least 25 lines and has structure beyond a single bullet list.
checks.gap: None. AGENTS.md is 59 lines with headed sections plus a routing table.

checks.expectation: The primary file is at most 400 lines.
checks.gap: None. Both root files are deliberately thin (59 and 47 lines) and delegate detail to .agents/.
checks.remediation: No action. Thin-entrypoint + .agents/ detail split is a model design.

checks.expectation: The instruction file states what the project is and who it is for in identifiable prose.
checks.gap: None. AGENTS.md 'What this repo is' states the purpose; CLAUDE.md restates the two showcases.

checks.expectation: Names the primary framework, language, and runtime.
checks.gap: None. TECH_STACK.md gives a one-owner-per-concern table; Node >=20 is in AGENTS.md and package.json engines.

checks.expectation: A brief architectural orientation: directory layout, dominant module pattern, or links to ARCHITECTURE.md.
checks.gap: None. ARCHITECTURE.md documents the feature-folder vertical-slice pattern; both root files route to it. Graph confirms via Feature-Folder Architecture and .agents Single Source of Truth nodes.

checks.expectation: Domain glossary present where domain terminology is used; canonical human-facing docs are linked.
checks.gap: None. GLOSSARY.md defines the ubiquitous language; root files cross-link README/ARCHITECTURE/ADRs/templates. No CONTRIBUTING.md exists, so its absence is not a gap.

checks.expectation: Lists canonical install, dev-server, test, and build commands.
checks.gap: None. AGENTS.md 'Run it' block documents install/setup/dev/verify/e2e; all resolve to real package.json scripts.

checks.expectation: States naming patterns, import-ordering, formatting, abbreviations policy.
checks.gap: None. CONVENTIONS.md is concrete and mechanical, including an identifier-scoped no-abbreviations rule with an accepted-acronym allowlist.

checks.expectation: States the commit-message convention and the branch-naming convention.
checks.gap: None. Conventional Commits enforced on commit-msg; subject form specified; a branch-naming convention is now documented at CONVENTIONS.md:59-64 (<type>/<short-kebab-summary>), closing the prior completeness note.

checks.expectation: States the testing convention: framework, query priority, behaviour-over-implementation, snapshot policy.
checks.gap: None. Jest + RTL behaviour-first with getByRole priority, userEvent over fireEvent, Playwright behaviour map as the contract, 80% coverage threshold.

checks.expectation: Explicit agent guidance with both positive and negative items.
checks.gap: None. AGENTS.md golden rules + Safety provide both positive and negative guidance.

checks.expectation: settings.json parses as JSON with no syntax errors.
checks.gap: None. Parses cleanly; pins a $schema.

checks.expectation: .gitignore excludes settings.local.json so personal overrides never leak into the shared project.
checks.gap: None — RESOLVED since the prior run. The root .gitignore now carries .claude/settings.local.json (line 34) and git check-ignore confirms the match. This was the sole violation in the previous audit.

checks.expectation: settings.json contains no secret-shaped values or secret-named keys with concrete values.
checks.gap: None. Only permission patterns and a hook command; no env block, no secret-named keys, no secret-shaped strings.

checks.expectation: permissions.allow contains no over-broad patterns beyond the threshold of 5.
checks.gap: None. All 19 allow entries are scoped to named pnpm scripts, scoped pnpm-exec invocations, or read-only git verbs plus git add. Zero broad patterns.

checks.expectation: Deployable projects forbid destructive commands in permissions.deny.
checks.gap: None. The deny list is non-empty and now covers four destructive verbs. The two extra entries (git reset --hard, git clean) were added since the prior run, closing the prior hardening note.

checks.expectation: Every hook command invokes a script that exists in the repository.
checks.gap: None. The PreToolUse(Bash) hook calls block-gate-bypass.sh, which exists, is executable, and blocks --no-verify with exit code 2. The hook now normalises whitespace (line 10) before pattern-matching, hardening it against the obfuscated-spacing bypass flagged in the prior run.

checks.expectation: No secret-valued env entries; MCP-server entries auditable; statusLine/model overrides intentional and current.
checks.gap: None / not-applicable. No env, mcpServers, .mcp.json, statusLine, or model override.

checks.expectation: When multiple instruction files exist, one is the source of truth and the others reference it.
checks.gap: None. CLAUDE.md opens 'Read AGENTS.md first'; both defer detail to .agents/. The graph's Agent Context Entry Points hyperedge and .agents Single Source of Truth node corroborate. No conflicting guidance found.

checks.expectation: Frameworks named in instruction files appear in package.json; no stale mentions.
checks.gap: None. React 19, Vite, Jest, RTL, Playwright, Biome, commitlint, husky, Fallow, StyleProof all present. No removed-tool mentions.

checks.expectation: Every script-running command in instruction files references a script that exists in package.json.
checks.gap: None. All documented commands resolve. pnpm install / pnpm exec / pnpm audit are not-applicable (not custom scripts).

checks.expectation: Internal references in instruction files point at files or directories that exist.
checks.gap: None. Every checked reference resolves on disk. New files (SECURITY.md, .fallowrc.jsonc, codeql/lighthouse/styleproof workflows) introduced no dangling references in the instruction corpus.

checks.expectation: Instruction files do not mention retired model IDs.
checks.gap: None. No model identifiers appear anywhere in the agentic corpus.

checks.expectation: In an active project, at least one instruction file was modified within 180 days.
checks.gap: None. Latest commit 2026-06-17; every instruction file modified 2026-06-17.

checks.expectation: When a 'no abbreviations' policy is declared, the instruction prose itself complies.
checks.gap: None — RESOLVED since the prior run. CONVENTIONS.md:14-16 now scopes the rule to identifiers and adds an accepted-acronym allowlist (E2E, CI, ADR, UI, IO, URL). The prose's acronym use is sanctioned by the policy itself; no self-compliance drift remains. This was the sole partial in the previous audit.
